intent: Provide a self-hosted Google-Drive-style cloud on this Arch PC: a dufs file server (web UI + WebDAV) exposed publicly via the existing Caddy+DuckDNS stack, with the KeePassXC vault auto-mirrored into it so it is reachable and syncable from the phone on mobile network.
scope: Non-goals: no second Caddy/DuckDNS updater, no hand-edit of /etc/nftables.conf, no new mobile app (WebDAV covers KeePassDX)
changes: Installer runs as the normal user with targeted sudo: installs a pinned, sha256-verified dufs v0.46.0 binary, writes a 0600 dufs.yaml (SHA-512 hashed auth, no anonymous access, bound to 127.0.0.1:5000), and installs+enables a system dufs.service.

Reuses the host exposure pattern from setup_gitea.sh: appends a marker-guarded reverse_proxy block for a new DuckDNS subdomain to the shared ~/gitea/Caddyfile and reloads gitea-caddy; extends the existing DuckDNS updater's domains= list (no new cron); opens 80/443 via setup_wireguard_ssh.sh allow-web only if not already open.

KeePass auto-sync: an event-driven systemd .path unit watches ~/Keepass/Passwords.kdbx and rsyncs ONLY that vault file into the served folder on every change (local .backup_* vaults stay off the public server); an initial mirror runs at install time.

Paranoid secret hardening: the plaintext password config is gitignored at 3 layers (repo root, linux_configuration/, **/ patterns); a new check-no-dufs-conf pre-commit hook blocks it even against `git add -f`; the installer refuses to write it unless `git check-ignore` confirms it is ignored; the password is hashed via openssl stdin (never in argv/ps); and a cloud-root guard forbids serving home/.ssh/.config/.gnupg/the repo/root.

verification.command: dufs v0.46.0 config + WebDAV smoke test (scratchpad, real binary)
verification.evidence: Flat-string auth 'user:$6$hash@/:rw' loads. no-auth->401, bad-pass->401, good->200; WebDAV PROPFIND->207, GET file->200 (returned content). Confirms schema, hashed auth, no anonymous access, WebDAV works.

DuckDNS domains sed adds label once and is skipped on re-run with token preserved; Caddy block appended once (1 cloud block) with tab indentation; unit rendering substitutes __USER__/__HOME__/__CLOUD_ROOT__ with no leftover placeholders.
No structural errors; only benign 'dufs not executable yet' (binary installed at deploy time) and sandbox 'unknown user' notes.

git check-ignore confirms .dufs_cloud.conf ignored at all 3 locations; check_no_dufs_conf.sh blocks a `git add -f`ed conf (exit 1) and cleanup leaves nothing staged; cloud-root guard rejects HOME/.ssh/.config/.gnupg/repo/`/`/`../` traversal and allows dedicated folders; openssl passwd -6 -stdin yields a $6$ hash without argv exposure.
Requires interactive password + sudo, so run by the user via '! ./setup_dufs_cloud.sh', plus a one-time manual DuckDNS subdomain registration. To be confirmed before commit.

risks: Public file server: mitigated by mandatory auth (SHA-512 hashed, no anonymous), HTTPS via Caddy, dufs bound to 127.0.0.1 only; the kdbx is itself master-password-encrypted.
Caddy block is appended to the shared Gitea Caddyfile; re-running setup_gitea.sh regenerates it and drops the block (re-run setup_dufs_cloud.sh to restore).
External reachability depends on a one-time manual DuckDNS subdomain creation and the existing router 80/443 forward.
rollback: sudo systemctl disable --now dufs.service keepass-cloud-sync.path; sudo rm /etc/systemd/system/{dufs.service,keepass-cloud-sync.service,keepass-cloud-sync.path}; sudo systemctl daemon-reload

Remove the cloud block from ~/gitea/Caddyfile and 'docker exec gitea-caddy caddy reload'; revert the domains= edit in ~/.joplin-server/duckdns-update.sh; sudo rm /usr/local/bin/dufs; rm -rf ~/.config/dufs ~/cloud
Validate after rollback: gitea still reachable at https://kuhy.duckdns.org and dufs.service is gone.
